title: Lathyrus sandbergii
intro: Lathyrus sandbergii, also known as Sandberg's pea or Sandberg's lathyrus, is a perennial flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular and digestive health, with potential benefits including lowering cholesterol and blood pressure, as well as alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus sandbergii include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Lathyrus sandbergii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create a cardiovascular-supportive blend.
taxonomy: Lathyrus sandbergii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with its specific classification being Lathyrus sandbergii (S. Watson) S. Watson in the family Fabaceae. This classification places Lathyrus sandbergii within the order Fabales and the genus Lathyrus. Further classification puts it in the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Loteae. The species is native to North America and is a perennial flowering plant. Its classification is consistent with its botanical characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Lathyrus sandbergii is a species of pea or grass pea that has been traditionally used for various medicinal purposes. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, Lathyrus sandbergii is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

side_effects: Lathyrus sandbergii is a plant that, like other plants in the Lathyrus genus, contains a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid). If ingested, ODAP can cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness, paralysis, and other neurological symptoms. The symptoms of neurolathyrism can be irreversible and even life-threatening in severe cases. Long-term consumption of Lathyrus sandbergii can also lead to birth defects and reproductive problems in both humans and animals. As with any potentially toxic plant, it is essential to exercise extreme caution when handling or consuming Lathyrus sandbergii.
